name: HRC_ARM_PROCESS_B
description: Approval model Table: This holds the process name mapped to each task available in the active composites and design time flags of whether various features like Rules Simulation, Archive, Bypass, Atomic transactions are supported by respective process.

columns.Name: PROCESS_ID
columns.Comments: Primary Key and unique identifier

columns.Name: IS_TASK_CONFIG_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: Flag to indicate if task config is supported

columns.Name: IS_COMMUNICATION_NTF_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: Flag to indicate that communication notification is supported or not

columns.Name: USE_COMMUNICATION_APPROVAL
columns.Comments: Flag indicates whether to use HCM communication for approvals

columns.Name: IS_AI_COMMENT_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: Design time flag whether the process supports AI comments feature or not

columns.Name: RESUBMIT_CALLBACK_CLASS
columns.Comments: The consumers can update the class in which a call back method will be written which is called at time of resubmit

columns.Name: IS_RULE_SIMULATION_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: Design time flag whether the process supports simulation feature or not

columns.Name: ARCHIVE_PERIOD
columns.Comments: This column is used to store the Archival Period of the Process.

columns.Name: RULE_CONFIGURE_MODE
columns.Comments: Will contain values null/External/BpmWorklist to identify which is the rule UI to be opened

columns.Name: APPROVAL_OVERRIDE_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: Design time flag whether the process supports administrator to provide override approval at any stage during the transaction life-cycle.

columns.Name: IS_TXN_FRAMEWORK_PROCESS
columns.Comments: Design time flag whether the process supports approvals by leveraging the transaction framework.

columns.Name: IS_COMMUNICATION_PROCESS
columns.Comments: Flag to indicate that process is specific to communication messages

columns.Name: CONTEXT_PARAM_FETCH_CALLBACK
columns.Comments: Used to fetch parameter context

columns.Name: RESOURCE_CONTEXT_SHORT_NAME
columns.Comments: This is FND Topology Module Short Name to get the context url of the Rest Service

columns.Name: REST_RESOURCE_KEY
columns.Comments: Represent the Column Attribute Name of the HRC_TXN_HEADER Table to pass the value as Primary Key to the Additional Rest Resource

columns.Name: ADDITIONAL_REST_RESOURCE
columns.Comments: Represents the additional Rest Resource Name

columns.Name: CATEGORY_CODE
columns.Comments: Grouping of approval process based on specified category within the application family

columns.Name: SUBCATEGORY_CODE
columns.Comments: Grouping of approval process based on specified sub category within the application family and category combination.

columns.Name: PRODUCT_CODE
columns.Comments: Grouping of approval process based on specified product code.

columns.Name: FAMILY
columns.Comments: The Application family that owns the approval process.

columns.Name: COMPOSITE_ID
columns.Comments: Foreign key to HRC_ARM_COMPOSITES

columns.Name: TASK_FILE_NAME
columns.Comments: Task file name associated to the parent process and available at the source

columns.Name: RULE_FILE_NAME
columns.Comments: Rule file name associated to the parent process and available at the source

columns.Name: ACTIVE
columns.Comments: Flag to identify if the process is active or inactive

columns.Name: ENTERPRISE_ID
columns.Comments:
Identify the ENTERPRISE  to which the process belongs.  
Foreign key to PER_ENTERPRISES.

columns.Name: MODULE_ID
columns.Comments: Seed Data Framework: indicates the module that owns the row. A module is an entry in Application Taxonomy such as a Logical Business Area. When the MODULE_ID column exists and the owner of the row is not specified, then the Seed Data Framework will not extract the row as seed data.

columns.Name: TXN_MODULE_IDENTIFIER
columns.Comments: The Module that sets up the transaction business data for the process.

columns.Name: CREATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who created the row.

columns.Name: CREATION_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the creation of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATED_BY
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_DATE
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the date and time of the last update of the row.

columns.Name: LAST_UPDATE_LOGIN
columns.Comments: Who column: indicates the session login associated to the user who last updated the row.

columns.Name: OBJECT_VERSION_NUMBER
columns.Comments: Used to implement optimistic locking. This number is incremented every time that the row is updated. The number is compared at the start and end of a transaction to detect whether another session has updated the row since it was queried.

columns.Name: SEED_DATA_SOURCE
columns.Comments: Source of seed data record. A value of 'BULK_SEED_DATA_SCRIPT' indicates that record was bulk loaded. Otherwise, specifies the name of the seed data file.

columns.Name: BYPASS_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: Design time flag whether the process supports bypassing approvals or not.

columns.Name: ASYNC_BYPASS_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: Used to identifiy which flow supports Async Bypass

columns.Name: TXN_SUB_MODULE_IDENTIFIER
columns.Comments: The Sub Module that sets up the transaction business data for the process.

columns.Name: IS_PARALLEL_TXN_SUPPORTED
columns.Comments: Design time flag whether the process supports Parallel Transactions.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET1
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET1. Context values are Y or N.

columns.Name: ORA_SEED_SET2
columns.Comments: Oracle internal use only. Indicates the edition-based redefinition (EBR) context of the row for SET2. Context values are Y or N.
